new_capabilities: Added repeatable --seed REG:OFFSET[,OFFSET...] support so multiple live object roots can be traced in the same function/window.
Kept the older --seed-register/--seed-path form for compatibility with previous automation and artifacts.
The scan payload now records normalized seed entries independently from the legacy single-seed fields.
tooling_milestone.why_added: The authCode producer uses two object roots at split-handler entry: RDI is the controller child and RDX is the incoming request/event object. A single seeded register could confirm child +0x38, but could not simultaneously show the request fields feeding that write.

split_entry_model.observation: Tracing the whole 0x14029E7C6 runtime function linearly sees RDX +0xD0 but misses RDX +0x80/+0x88 because the later block is not a normal fallthrough from the early dispatch path.
split_entry_model.resolution: Treat 0x14029E7C6..0x14029E83B and 0x14029E894..0x14029E970 as separate explicit seeded windows inside the same runtime function.
split_entry_model.bounded_inference: 0x14029E894 is a split sub-entry or continuation body reached with the original incoming RDX request/event object still live.

seeds.meaning: Controller child object whose +0x38 slot receives the authCode/OTL continuation string.
Incoming request/event object used as the source for URL and authCode string material.

key_events.interpretation: The early dispatch path builds or opens a small-string source rooted at request/event +0xD0.
The controller child reaches its service source through child +0x60, matching the previous client +0x536B8 service path.
The authCode builder reads the request/event start pointer or begin field from +0x80.
The authCode builder reads the paired request/event end pointer or limit field from +0x88, then subtracts +0x80 to derive a length.
The derived authCode string is copied/built into controller child +0x38.

confirmed: The authCode/OTL producer has two relevant split-entry source regions: request/event +0xD0 for the early URL/open path and request/event +0x80/+0x88 for the authCode string builder.
The controller child destination remains child +0x38, reached from client +0x198D0 -> +0x110.
The +0x80/+0x88 pair behaves like a begin/end pointer pair because the code loads both fields and computes RAX - RCX before constructing the temporary string.
reversing_result.opennxt_relevance: For OpenNXT 947, this narrows the native client's web-login continuation model: the server-side auth/web handoff likely needs to supply data that becomes the request/event +0x80/+0x88 source span and ultimately child +0x38, while the actual game socket remains a separate login/JS5 compatibility problem.

next_targets: Map the predecessor branch/state block that enters 0x14029E894 with RDI and RDX live.
Scan neighboring split sub-entries for the same RDI/RDX seed pattern to build a controller-child state transition table.
Add optional basic-block or branch-entry awareness to pe-object-field-trace so split sub-entry windows can be discovered rather than supplied manually.
